..summary: SharedExploreParams (package-private record in api/) carries the same three fields as ExplorationOptions (domain model) with identical semantics, creating a permanent translation layer between the two with no additional policy

evidence: SharedExploreParams: topCollocates, minShared, logDiceThreshold
ExplorationOptions: topCollocates, minShared, logDiceThreshold — identical field names and semantics
ExplorationHandlers parses HTTP params into SharedExploreParams, then constructs ExplorationOptions from it — two objects are created that carry the same data
detail.suggestion: Consider whether SharedExploreParams can be eliminated by having the HTTP parsing code directly construct ExplorationOptions. If HTTP-layer concerns (e.g. default values, parameter name aliases) require separation, document the rationale so the duplication is intentional.

..summary: HttpApiUtils.warnIfWildcardCors has a multi-paragraph Javadoc that restates the implementation logic visible a few lines below

evidence: Javadoc reads: 'Wildcard CORS must be an explicit opt-in: set the system property allow.wildcard.cors=true to suppress the error and allow all origins. Without that property, a wildcard origin fails fast to prevent accidental exposure in production.'
The method body (8 lines) performs exactly what the Javadoc describes: checks System.getProperty('allow.wildcard.cors'), branches on true/false, throws with the same error message as in the Javadoc
detail.suggestion: Trim the Javadoc to a single sentence describing intent ('Fails fast if CORS allow-origin is set to wildcard without explicit opt-in.'), removing the inline re-statement of the branching logic.

..summary: @NonNull seeds parameter in exploreMultiSeed() contradicted by explicit null-check in body

evidence: Method signature: public @NonNull ExplorationResult exploreMultiSeed(@NonNull Set<String> seeds, ...)
Line 207: if (seeds == null || seeds.size() < 2) — null branch can never execute under the declared contract, creating dead-code confusion
detail.suggestion: Remove the null branch and keep only the size check: if (seeds.size() < 2). If null input is a real concern, drop the @NonNull annotation and document the null behaviour.

..summary: CollocateQueryHelper accepts null index with a verbal-only behavioural contract that the type system cannot enforce

evidence: Constructor: CollocateQueryHelper(@Nullable BlackLabIndex index) — Javadoc says 'Callers must not invoke any method that delegates to blackLabIndex when those are null'
BlackLabQueryExecutor test constructor uses @SuppressWarnings("NullAway") to silence the resulting warning
getTotalFrequency, performCollocateSearch all dereference index unconditionally after accepting it as @Nullable
detail.suggestion: Replace the nullable-index test pattern with a dedicated test subclass or a mock that overrides I/O methods. The production constructor should take a non-null index and the @Nullable annotation should be removed.

..summary: ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry and ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry are parallel nested records representing the same graph-edge concept with identical fields (source, target, log_dice, type), defined redundantly in two sibling response classes

evidence: ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry: source, target, log_dice (double), type (RelationEdgeType)
ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry: source, target, log_dice (double), type (RelationEdgeType)
Both records appear in JSON responses with identical field shapes and the same RelationEdgeType enum values
detail.suggestion: Extract a shared EdgeEntry record into api/model/ (or reuse the domain model Edge class) and reference it from both ComparisonResponse and ExploreResponse. Alternatively keep the duplication and document the intentional divergence.

..summary: SeedCollocateEntry is a top-level public record in api/model/ while CollocateProfileEntry, CoreCollocateEntry, DiscoveredNounEntry and EdgeEntry are nested private records inside their owning response class — inconsistent nesting strategy across sibling files

evidence: SeedCollocateEntry is a standalone public record in api/model/
DiscoveredNounEntry, CoreCollocateEntry, EdgeEntry, Parameters are all nested inside ExploreResponse
CollocateProfileEntry and EdgeEntry are nested inside ComparisonResponse
SeedCollocateEntry is imported from api/model.ExploreResponse.java as a top-level type while sibling entry types are nested
detail.suggestion: Decide one strategy: either make all sub-entry records nested (private) inside their owning response class, or promote all of them to top-level records in api/model/. The nested approach is more common in Java record-based response models.

..summary: BlackLab 4.x not published to Maven Central; requires manual local-install script and a raw-GitHub-URL fallback with no SLA

evidence: pom.xml repositories block: 'BlackLab 4.x is NOT published to Maven Central (latest on Central is 3.0.1). REQUIRED FIRST STEP: run ./install-blacklab.sh'
blacklab-github-fallback profile uses 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/…' with checksumPolicy=warn and comment 'DO NOT rely on it in production CI'
detail.suggestion: Mirror the BlackLab 4.x JARs to a private registry (Nexus, Artifactory, or GitHub Packages) so the build is reproducible without an out-of-band install step and without depending on a raw GitHub URL.

..summary: ExploreResponseAssembler.ExamplesContext uses a @Nullable Boolean as a tri-state fallback flag

evidence: ExamplesContext record has field: @Nullable Boolean fallback — null means 'not a fallback', true means 'fallback was used'
Call site in ConcordanceHandlers passes fallback ? Boolean.TRUE : null, which is semantically Optional<Boolean>
This pattern forces callers to box a primitive boolean and treat null as a meaningful third value
detail.suggestion: Replace @Nullable Boolean fallback with boolean fallback (primitive, defaults to false) or with Optional<String> fallbackReason if the reason matters to callers. This eliminates the null-as-false semantic and makes the intent explicit.

..summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern passes a long multi-branch inline lambda that requires a sentinel comment to explain the -1 value, making the call-site hard to read

evidence: The lambda passed to collectFrequenciesAndPosFromGroups contains three distinct branches guarded by a 'Uses < 0 to handle any negative sentinel value' comment and two fallback paths (XML parse then plain-text fallback)
The sentinel -1 meaning ('label 2: absent from pattern') is established in CqlUtils.findLabelTokenIndex but must be remembered at the call site
detail.suggestion: Extract the lambda body into a named private method (e.g. extractCollocateFromIdentity(identity, collocatePos)) that encapsulates the sentinel check and fallback logic, removing the need for an inline comment explaining the control flow.

..summary: MultiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection performs a redundant extra pass converting List<WordSketchResult> to Map<String,Double> after aggregation is already complete

evidence: Lines after aggregateCollocateStats() build perSeedCollocates by iterating over data.seedCollocateMap() a second time — the same collection already traversed inside fetchCollocatesPerSeed and aggregateCollocateStats
The conversion (List<WordSketchResult> → Map<String,Double>) discards the WordSketchResult type without any filtering or transformation logic
detail.suggestion: Build perSeedCollocates inside fetchCollocatesPerSeed as a parallel Map<String,Map<String,Double>>, or return it directly from SeedCollocateData alongside the existing seedCollocateMap, eliminating the post-aggregation loop.
